data.title: Stocks gain after mild US inflation; yields buoyed by trade tensions
data.description:
Key points:
  • Beaten-down tech shares lead rebound in U.S. and Asia
  • Trump's back and forth on tariffs weighs on sentiment
  • Uncertainty lifts gold to within $13 of record high

Tech stocks led advances in Asia on Thursday, taking their cue from Wall Street's gains after tepid inflation data allayed concerns over the state of the U.S. economy.

U.S. Treasury yields remained elevated after pulling further away from recent lows the day before, on escalating tit-for-tat tariff battles between the United States and trading partners.

The euro was steady after slipping back from a five-month high on Wednesday, when President Donald Trump warned of a response to the European Union's threatened counter-tariffs on U.S. goods. However, the shared currency continued to garner support from signs of progress towards peace between Russia and Ukraine.

Gold climbed to within $13 of its record peak, while crude oil was stable following a 2% climb in the prior session on the back of a smaller-than-expected rise in U.S. stockpiles.

Japan's Nikkei gained 0.9%, buoyed by advances in chip-sector heavyweights such as Advantest and Tokyo Electron.

Taiwan's tech-heavy equities index TWSE:TAIEX added 0.6%, and South Korea's KOSPI climbed 0.7%

Mainland Chinese blue chips edged up 0.1%, although Hong Kong's Hang Seng retreated from small early gains to be down 0.3%.

The U.S. S&P 500 advanced 0.5% on Wednesday, and the Nasdaq jumped 1.2%, with beaten-down megacap tech shares rebounding sharply. Futures in both indexes , pointed 0.1% higher on Thursday.

On Wednesday, data showed U.S. consumer prices rose 0.2% last month after accelerating 0.5% in January. Excluding the volatile food and energy components, the CPI climbed 0.2% in February after gaining 0.4% in January.

The inflation figures were closely watched following a run of softer economic data recently and the potential inflationary impact of Trump's tariff campaign.

However, analysts noted the components that were mainly responsible for the cooling in price pressures will not feed into the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index. Producer prices due later on Thursday will also bear close scrutiny.

"Uncertainty remains in the air as the outlook for consumer price inflation remains blurred by trade policy developments," TD Securities analysts wrote in a client note.

"The impact from the recent implementation of tariffs on Chinese, Canadian and Mexican goods and the expectation of further announcements suggest the worst is yet to come."

Trump's increased tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminium imports took effect on Wednesday, stepping up a campaign to reorder global trade in favour of the U.S. and drawing swift retaliation from Canada and Europe.

Trade uncertainty saw U.S. Treasury yields extend a climb from Tuesday's five-month low for two-year notes overnight. The yield (US2YT=RR) last stood at 3.924% after rising as high as 4.005% on Wednesday. It sank as low as 3.829% on Tuesday for the first time since October 4.

Rising yields supported the dollar, which was steady at $1.0895 per euro on Thursday, after weakening to $1.0947 on Tuesday for the first time since October 11.

Europe's shared currency has soared on the promise of bigger spending on defence and infrastructure by Germany, along with progress towards a ceasefire in the Ukraine war.

Meanwhile, the dollar has been on the defensive with Trump's unpredictable trade policies sowing concerns about a possible recession in the United States.

The safe-haven yen pulled back to 146.205, after reaching its strongest since October 4 at 146.545 per dollar on Tuesday.

A steep climb in 30-year Japanese government bond yields (JP30YTN=JBTC) also faded. Yields sank as low as 2.53% on Thursday, easing from their highest since 2006 at 2.615% on Wednesday.

Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda said on Wednesday that recent rises in yields were a natural reflection of market expectations of future interest rate hikes, underscoring the central bank's resolve to continue tightening monetary policy.

Gold rose about 0.3% to as high as $2,943.49, closing in on the record high from February 24 at $2,956.15.

Crude oil steadied following Wednesday's rally. Brent futures eased 0.1% to $70.88 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures edged down 0.2% to $67.57 a barrel.

Cryptocurrency bitcoin added 1% to around $84,000, making up additional ground following its sharp drop to a four-month trough at $76,666.98 on Tuesday.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.5% Higher at 74392.54 on Broad-Based Gains
Baidu Completes Issuance of 10 Billion Yuan Bonds
data.description:
Baidu completed the issuance of 10 billion yuan of bonds consisting of two tranches on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The offering comprised 7.5 billion yuan of 2.70% senior notes due 2030, and 2.5 billion yuan of 3.0% senior notes due 2035.

The bonds are expected to be listed on the Hong Kong bourse on March 13, a separate filing said.

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Gains
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar rose to around $0.573 on Thursday as the U.S. dollar remained subdued amid growing concerns about U.S. trade policies.

While weaker-than-expected U.S. consumer inflation provided some relief to investors, worries about the potential impact of intensifying tariff wars on economic growth continue to weigh on sentiment.

This comes after President Donald Trump pledged to impose more tariffs after his latest move to introduce import taxes on steel and aluminium prompted retaliation from the EU and Canada.

However, the Kiwi’s gains were limited by concerns over persistent deflationary pressures in China, New Zealand’s largest trading partner, which could dampen demand for the country’s exports.

Domestically, the currency remains under pressure as RBNZ signaled further rate cuts in its last policy meeting, though it emphasized a more cautious approach to future moves.

data.title: Samsung Electronics, Nvidia Partner to Advance AI-RAN for Mobile Networks
data.description:
Samsung Electronics partnered with US-based technology firm Nvidia to enhance AI-RAN technologies, the South Korean electronics products maker said Wednesday.

The collaboration will boost Samsung's ecosystem by integrating both the central processing unit and graphics processing unit capabilities.

Samsung plans to integrate its vRAN with Nvidia's computing into commercial off-the-shelf servers, enabling AI-RAN deployment, the release said.

data.title: Omega Oil & Gas Completes Fracture Stimulation at Queensland Well; Shares Reach All-Time High
data.description:
Omega Oil & Gas completed the fracture stimulation program at its Canyon-1H horizontal well in the Taroom Trough in Queensland's Bowen Basin within its estimated schedule, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The cleanup phase of the flow test is now underway, with gas and condensate flows from the stimulated zone in Canyon-1H flowing to the surface in combination with the fracture fluid, the filing said.

The flow testing program is expected to last about 30 days, according to the filing.

Shares fell past 2% in recent trade Thursday after earlier reaching an all-time high.

data.title: Corn loses more ground on US inventories, trade war
data.description:
Key points:
  • Corn firms, but U.S. stock, trade war limit gains
  • Soybeans rise after four sessions of losses; wheat gains

Chicago corn futures ticked up on Thursday, recouping some of the previous session's losses, although gains were limited by higher U.S. stocks and worries over tariffs disrupting trade flows.

Soybeans rose for the first time in five sessions, while wheat climbed.

The most-active corn contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) rose 0.1% to $4.61 a bushel as of 0320 GMT. Soybeans added 0.4% to $10.04-1/2 a bushel and wheat gained 0.6% to $5.57-1/4 a bushel.

U.S. President Donald Trump's increased tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminium imports took effect on Wednesday, stepping up a campaign to reorder global trade in favour of the U.S. and drawing swift retaliation from Canada and Europe.

The European Union announced counter-tariffs on up to 26 billion euros ($28 billion) worth of U.S. goods from next month, including agricultural products such as soybeans, almonds and pork.

Canada, the biggest foreign supplier of steel and aluminium to the United States, announced 25% retaliatory tariffs on those metals along with computers, sports equipment and other products worth C$29.8 billion ($20.72 billion) in total.

Canadian farmers intend to plant more wheat, corn, oats and peas in 2025 than they did last year and cut canola, soybeans, barley and lentils, Statistics Canada reported on Wednesday.

The U.S. government left domestic corn inventories unchanged in a monthly supply-and-demand report, despite strong export sales.

Farm office FranceAgriMer on Wednesday cut its forecast of French soft wheat exports outside the European Union in 2024-25 to 3.2 million metric tons, down 69% on the previous season and marking a new low this century after a rain-hit crop.

Commodity funds net sold CBOT corn, soymeal and soyoil futures contracts on Wednesday, traders said. Funds were net even in soybean and wheat futures, traders said. (COMFUND/CBT)

($1 = C$1.4384)

data.title: Ayala Land's 2025-2026 Earnings Growth Likely to Stay Strong — Market Talk
data.description: Ayala Land's 2025-2026 earnings growth is likely to remain strong, Maybank Securities' Raffy Mendoza says in a research report as the brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating. One driver is the property developer's residential segment, as it will continue to depend on demand from its proven market leadership across its premium brands, the analyst says. Another driver is the Philippine company's mall retail business, which should capture resilient domestic consumption, the analyst adds. However, the brokerage trims its 2025 and 2026 earnings forecasts for Ayala Land by 2% and 4%, respectively, to align with the management's guidance. It lowers the stock's target price to PHP40.00 from PHP43.80. Shares are 1.35% lower at PHP22.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Naga Dhunseri Acquires Additional Stake in Dhunseri Tea & Industries
data.description:
Dhunseri Tea & Industries completed the sale of an additional 45.77% stake in the company to Naga Dhunseri Group , making it a subsidiary of the latter, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

In November 2024, Naga Dhunseri had agreed to acquire 4,809,595 equity shares constituting 45.77% stake of Dhunseri Tea from an associate company Dhunseri Investments.

Naga Dhunseri now holds 54.56% stake in Dhunseri Tea.

data.title: IndusInd Bank Crisis: RBI steps in, tells board to nominate external candidates for COO, CEO posts
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of India has told the board of crisis-hit IndusInd Bank to look for external candidates for the post of COO and CEO, an Economic Times report said on Thursday.

The development comes a day after the central bank launched a sector-wide assessment of the derivative positions. According to the ET report, the RBI has told the bank board to nominate two candidates for the top position who are not part of the financial institution.

It should be noted that last week, the RBI had given nod to a year-long extension to the reappointment of current chief executive officer Sumant Kathpalia.

His term was originally supposed to end on March 24 this year. However, after the extension, Kathapalia’s term will now end in March next year. So far, there ahs been no official announcement by either RBI or IndusInd bank on the nomination of the external candidates.

Previously, in an analyst call, Kathapalia had said that the central bank may have some concerns regarding his leadership skills.

On being given just a year of extension, the current IndusInd CEO had said that the RBI was ‘uncomfortable’ with his style of working.

Crisis ensued at India’s fifth largest bank after it disclosed discrepancies in account balances. In a statement on Monday, the bank had said that an internal review was on to assess the position of derivative portfolio. The bank had said that this could lead to 2.35% hit to its net worth.A day later, on March 11, the stocks of IndusInd Bank plummeted 27 percent. Meanwhile, brokerage firm CLSA has given an 'Outperform' tag to IndusInd Bank. However, it has lowered the price target from to Rs 900 apiece.

data.title: Traders could be prepping to buy as USDT activity hits 6-month high
data.description:
Onchain activity for Tether has hit a sixth-month high, possibly indicating traders are gearing up to jump back into the market, according to analysts.

Data shared by the blockchain data platform Santiment in a March 12 X post shows Tether’s onchain activity has been on the rise, peaking with over 143,000 wallets making transfers on March 11, the highest in six months.

“When USDT & other stablecoin activity spikes during price drops, traders are preparing to buy. Added buy pressure aids in crypto prices recovering,” Santiment said.

It comes as Bitcoin dropped to a four-month low of $76,700 on March 11, as the wider crypto market shed even more of the gains made post-US election amid macroeconomic uncertainty and an escalating tariff war.

Speaking to Cointelegraph, Vincent Liu, chief investment officer at Kronos Research, said traders often accumulate Tether during dips to position themselves for buying opportunities, adding buy pressure that can help crypto prices recover.

He speculates the uptick in USDT wallet activity likely reflects traders capitalizing on recent market volatility.

“Possible causes include broader economic uncertainties, crypto-specific events like regulatory developments or post-election sentiment shifts, and Tether’s role as a stable haven, making it an ideal holding for investors preparing to deploy capital strategically,” Liu said.

Liu says the surge in USDT activity is a bullish indicator, suggesting significant buying power on the sidelines, but the crypto market’s recovery will likely depend on factors like macroeconomic conditions, regulatory clarity, and investor confidence.

“However, with the inflation rate easing to 2.8% in February, lower than expected in recent CPI data, this could reduce pressure on crypto prices and signal a more favorable environment,” he said.

“Additionally, the upcoming Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) on March 18th may provide further indications on interest rates and monetary policy, potentially influencing market development and recovery,” Liu added. 

A key Bitcoin and crypto sentiment tracker, the Crypto Fear & Greed Index, hit its lowest score in over two years on Feb. 26 as it slipped deeper into “Extreme Fear,” reaching a score of 10.

Crypto sentiment has staged a recovery since, but the index has still registered a score of 45 on March 13, still in fear territory.

Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino touring the US 

Meanwhile, Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino is currently on a tour of the US as lawmakers move to regulate the sector.

During a March 12 speech at the Cantor Fitzgerald Global Technology Conference, he said that as it stands, around 37% of USDT users are using it as a savings account to store value.

“They don’t have bank accounts. The only thing that they have in their life is usually cash,” Ardoino said.

“Now they finally can hold the most used and most important stable currency in the world, that is the US dollar, but they keep it in their smartphones as their savings account.”

Cantor
@Official_Cantor

CEO of @Tether_to, @paoloardoino, speaks at Cantor's Global Tech Conference. #CantorTechhttps://t.co/2z8d46WDMG

Mar 12, 2025

At the same time, Ardoino said, Tether is acting as one of the “last strongholds for the US dollar” amid growing concerns that the US dollar could lose dominance as the world’s reserve currency and a go-to for international transactions and commodity trades.

The stablecoin issuer has also been working to curb bad actors in the space, collaborating on more than 170 law enforcement operations and freezing $2.5 billion in illicit funds, according to Ardoino.

Magazine: Crypto fans are obsessed with longevity and biohacking: Here’s why

data.title: Market Chatter: Alibaba Introduces Emotional Intelligence Model
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding rolled out an artificial intelligence model that could read emotions, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday.

The R1-Omni is said to detect someone's emotional state through video and could describe their clothes and environment, the report said, citing demonstrations by researchers at Alibaba's Tongyi Lab.

Alibaba's new model is seen as a bid to outrun OpenAI, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Samsonite rises on annual profit beat, higher dividend
data.description:
** Shares of luggage bags manufacturer Samsonite Group SA rise 3.6% to HK$20.2, on track for their biggest one-day gain since December 23, 2024

** Stock set to snap three straight sessions of fall

** Luxembourg-based company said its 2024 profit attributable to the equity holders fell 12.9% y/y to $345.6 million, compared with an average forecast of about $339 mln profit for the year

** Co said net sales slid 2.5% y/y to $3.59 billion and it declared a final dividend of $0.1074 per share vs $0.1026 each in the previous year

** Hang Seng Consumption Index (.HSCGSI) falls 1.2%, Hang Seng Index slips 0.3%

** Stock down 8.4% YTD

data.title: Singapore Dollar Strengthens Slightly Amid Lower Treasury Yields — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar strengthens slightly against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session amid lower Treasury yields that reduce the appeal of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. Appetite for risky assets may also have improved after U.S. inflation data released overnight were cooler than expected, analysts say. The figures have "helped appetite for risk lean forward and detach itself from the ropes it had pinned to," says Matt Simpson, market analyst at Forex.com and City Index, in a commentary. USD/SGD edges 0.1% lower to 1.3320. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield is down 2 bps at 4.2994%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: TRUMP Token Takedown—Did Insiders Plan The Crash?
data.description:
The official TRUMP token, designed to commemorate US President Donald Trump’s second presidency, was an instant hit upon launch. The token surged from less than $10 on January 18th to a high of $74.59 on January 20th before quickly surrendering some gains within hours.

Although the token remained competitive days after the president’s inauguration, by trading above $30, it quickly faded under pressure. TRUMP dipped below $20 on February 2nd, and it’s now trading at $10. 

Observers say that the broader crypto market and TRUMP’s token are crashing. However, TRUMP’s crash is not without controversy—10x Research has disclosed that there was “insider play” before the coin’s massive drop. While most traders lost billions of dollars during its crash, a sizeable number of early investors cashed in for huge profits.

Early Investors Cash Out Before Listing In Major Exchanges

According to 10x Research, most early investors cashed in just before major exchanges listed the coin, as its value moved past $60 and briefly hit $70. The coin’s rapid surge in price was welcomed by everyone, with the early investors getting the best seats in the house. After its quick rise, TRUMP suffered a massive drop. From the low $20s, it’s now trading at $10, leaving retail and small traders with losses.

10x Research
@10x_Research

The $TRUMP Dump: When the Hype Fades, Reality Hits

1-4) A clear example is the $TRUMP coin, where insiders and those with early access at the Washington crypto ball could buy in before the public, while exchanges rushed to list the token as it soared past $60. After briefly… pic.twitter.com/PVzLcVbL0m

Mar 11, 2025

Losses related to the TRUMP price drop are reminiscent of previous bearish cycles, including the 2021 NFTs boom and bust. With the TRUMP token, the value significantly dropped within the week. By looking at the bigger picture, TRUMP token shed more than 80% since its peak last January. And on-chain data suggests that early investors quickly liquidated their positions, with retail and small traders used as pawns.

Solana Chain Takes A Hit

The Solana ecosystem is one of the biggest losers in the token’s crash. Aside from TRUMP, a few other Solana-based tokens faced selling pressure, including Raydium’s RAY token, which dropped by 60% in the last month.

Even Solana’s native token, SOL, dropped by over 40% over the same period. The drop in value for SOL-based tokens suggests that the interest in meme coins or speculative tokens is waning.Pump.fun Also Sees Dramatic Shift

Pump.fun, another leading meme coin platform, has also witnessed a dramatic drop in network activity. Over the past year, this platform processed 8.4 million meme coin launches, peaking days ahead of Trump’s inauguration.

From Christmas season to early January, up to 1.7 million meme tokens were launched on Pump.fun. However, the daily launches on Pump.fun have dropped. Also, participation rates have fallen on this platform, reflecting declining interest on this asset class.

Feature image from Newsweek, chart from TradingView

data.title: Seek Keeps Bull Despite Moderated Earnings Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Australian jobs advertiser Seek keeps its bull at Barrenjoey despite the investment bank's analyst moderating his earnings expectations. Analyst Eric Choi tells clients in a note that he thinks the implementation of the so-called freemium model at Seek's Asia business could lead to flat revenue growth into fiscal 2026. He also lowers his fiscal 2026 volume expectations for Australia and New Zealand to reflect Barrenjoey economists' view of slowing overall employment growth. However, he thinks that Seek is committed to operating leverage through cost management and still sees annual EPS growth of 19%-23% for the three years through fiscal 2028. Barrenjoey stays overweight on the stock, which is up 1.7% at A$22.74. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0332 GMT - Australian jobs advertiser Seek keeps its bull at Barrenjoey despite the investment bank's analyst moderating his earnings expectations. Analyst Eric Choi tells clients in a note that he thinks the implementation of the so-called freemium model at Seek's Asia business could lead to flat revenue growth into fiscal 2026. He also lowers his fiscal 2026 volume expectations for Australia and New Zealand to reflect Barrenjoey economists' view of slowing overall employment growth. However, he thinks that Seek is committed to operating leverage through cost management and still sees annual EPS growth of 19%-23% for the three years through fiscal 2028. Barrenjoey stays overweight on the stock, which is up 1.7% at A$22.74. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0314 GMT - Crude palm oil prices should remain supported by Malaysia's supply tightness in the near term, say UOB Kay Hian analysts in a note. Malaysia's palm oil stocks fell 4.3% on month in February, according to the Malaysian Palm Oil Board. However, factors such as the seasonal ramp-up in CPO production could begin exerting downward pressure on prices in the coming months, they say. UOB Kay Hian maintains a market weight rating on Malaysia's plantation sector. The analysts have a buy rating on Hap Seng Plantations and SD Guthrie, as they continue to like these companies for their upstream business, favorable production trends, and high dividend yields.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0307 GMT - Bermaz Auto will likely face a challenging outlook in fiscal 2025 due to increasing competition from Chinese-made vehicles, says TA Securities analyst Angeline Chin in a note. These vehicles are competitively priced and boast advanced connectivity features, she writes. The company holds the electric-vehicle distributorship for Deepal, which has a new model slated to be launched in Malaysia by 4Q, she notes. That should help diversify and expand its product portfolio for new customer segments, she adds. However, Chin doesn't expect significant contributions from Deepal, given the many EV options available, which makes the segment highly competitive. TA Securities has a sell rating on the stock and a target price of MYR0.94. Shares are at MYR1.02. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0247 GMT - Stecon Group may deliver a robust earnings turnaround in 2025, Thanachart Securities' Saksid Phadthananarak says in a research report as the brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating. The construction company's backlog at end-2024 totaled THB89 billion, and it awaits contract signing for two other projects, the analyst notes. The Thai company's new work value could reach THB40 billion in 2025, THB30 billion in 2026 and THB15 billion in 2027, the analyst says. Other earnings drivers are its lack of unprofitable projects and the absence of loss contribution from the pink and yellow MRT lines. However, the brokerage lowers the stock's target price to THB9.00 from THB11.00 to reflect a higher discount rate. Shares last closed 1.8% lower at THB5.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0229 GMT - Naver's commission adjustments, effective June 2, are set to boost revenue and earnings, Nomura analysts Angela Hong and Won Kang write in a note. The South Korean internet platform giant's new take-rate structure of reduced commissions can lead sellers to spend more on advertising, they say. The company has room to improve its e-commerce monetization given its low rates of commissions to sales, they note. They raise their earnings-per-share estimates for Naver by 4%-15% for 2025-2026. Nomura upgrades its rating on the stock to buy from neutral and raises its target price by 18% to KRW260,000. Shares are 1.4% higher at KRW217,000. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0226 GMT - Delfi's growth in 2025 is likely to be dragged by elevated cocoa prices, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report. They lower the stock's target price to S$0.82 from S$0.86, with an unchanged hold rating. Cocoa prices remain much higher than they have historically been owing to persistent supply issues, the analysts note. If the weather continues to be unfavorable in cocoa-producing regions, price increases are likely, they say. The brokerage cuts its 2025-2026 Ebitda margin forecasts for the chocolate confectionery product maker by 1-2 percentage points on expectations selling and distribution costs will remain high due to continued stiff competition. Shares fall 2.0% to S$0.745. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0150 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index falls 0.4% to 23499.48, erasing opening gains amid worries over a global trade war. With President Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum kicking in with no exemptions, the risk of a tit-for-tat tariff escalation is now more pronounced, says Vishnu Varathan, head of Macro Research for Asia ex-Japan at Mizuho Securities (Singapore). Among the worst performers on the benchmark index, Anta Sports falls 2.7%, Kuaishou Technology loses 2.2% and New Oriental Education & Technology Group is down 2.4%. Meanwhile, Li Auto rises 2.2% and Chow Tai Fook Jewellery adds 2.6%. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 0.8% lower at 5799.22. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0145 GMT - Chinese shares are lower in volatile early trade. "Global markets will remain on tenterhooks as sentiment remains fragile from rising tariff barriers," OCBC analysts say in a note. Resource stocks are leading the gains. China Shenhua Energy adds 0.45% and PetroChina is 0.5% higher. Software stocks are broadly lower, with Beijing Kingsoft Office Software down 0.2% and 360 Security Technology falling 1.1%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.1% lower at 3369,62, the Shenzhen Composite Index is down 0.5% and the ChiNext Price Index is off 0.2%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0120 GMT - There are multiple reasons why Westpac is likely to underperform the broader Australian share market in 2025, according to the bank's bears at Morgan Stanley. They highlight eight reasons for their continued underweight rating, but point to three as the most important: margin trends, capital management and the lender's ongoing tech overhaul. The analysts point out in a note to clients that margins and capital both fell short of expectations in Westpac's first fiscal quarter, while investors are waiting on more detail about the tech overhaul benefits. Raising their expense estimates, the MS analysts cut their target price by 6.5% to A$27.30. Shares are down 1.3% at A$29.68. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0114 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% lower to 3828.84 as investors digest mixed developments. Main theme appears to be relief after U.S. CPI data released Wednesday were slightly below consensus expectations, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. However, there have also been fears of an escalation in the trade war, the analysts add, noting that the EU and Canada announced retaliatory tariffs against the U.S. overnight. Among the worst performers on the benchmark index, Yangzijiang Shipbuilding falls 2.6%, DFI Retail Group loses 1.75%, and Mapletree Industrial Trust is down 1.5%. Meanwhile, City Developments rises 3.6% and Hongkong Land adds 1.8%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0113 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is up 0.4% at 1490.42 in early trade. While broad-based selling pressure has intensified on the bourse recently, sentiment is likely to improve today, supported by U.S. stocks' overnight rebound, analysts at Apex Securities write in a note. Bargain hunting activities could emerge but gains may be tepid given the absence of fresh leads, as global uncertainties continue to dominate, they add. Among the advancers, Gamuda rises 2.4%, YTL Power International gains 2.0% and Sunway is 1.7% higher. Meanwhile, Axiata Group and CelcomDigi each fall 1.7%, and CIMB Group is 0.6% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0106 GMT - Continued cost-of-living pressures and economic uncertainty keep Macquarie analysts cautious on Premier Investments following its divestment of several clothing businesses. The analysts write in a note that the discretionary nature and relatively high price point of the conglomerate's remaining Smiggle and Peter Alexander brands mean they are exposed to the impact of inflation and elevated interest rates on consumers. They expect Premier to report first-half Ebit slightly above the midpoint of its A$160 million-A$165 million guidance range. Macquarie cuts its target price 26% to A$21.60 but keeps a neutral rating on the stock, which is down 1.7% at A$20.94. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Coolabah Metals' Unit to Replace NSW Mine Bond
data.description:
Coolabah Metals said unit Broken Hill Mines Pty Ltd signed a conditional term sheet with a North American insurer for a surety bond to replace the existing AU$17 million cash-backed environmental bond over the Rasp Mine in New South Wales, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The surety bond offers a four-year, AU$17 million facility with a 2.5% interest rate, and is progressively cash covered over the term, subject to approvals, the filing said.

The bond will allow the unit to use previously held cash for growth activities at the Rasp and Pinnacles mines, as well as general working capital, per the filing.

data.title: Australia, NZ dlrs camped out as US dollar wobbles
data.description:
The Australian and New Zealand dollars sat in the top half of well-established ranges on Wednesday, as a wobbly U.S. dollar offsets concern about a slowdown in global trade.

The Aussie , which is historically sensitive to global growth and trade thanks to Australia's commodity exports, found support at $0.63 and bought $0.6320 in afternoon trade.

The kiwi dollar held steady at $0.5733 for a gain so far in March of 2.4% on the U.S. dollar.

Neither has broken free of a roughly three-cent band that has contained them this year, even as global markets have been roiled by the reversal of bets on a stronger U.S. dollar as the U.S. has imposed import tariffs.

"It's a pretty tight range given how much has been going on," said Su-Lin Ong, chief economist at RBC Capital Markets in Sydney, as the Aussie has navigated an Australian rate cut, some strong domestic data and a rollercoaster few weeks in markets.

"There's obviously a confluence of factors pulling in multiple directions," she said.

Fears of a sharp economic slowdown in the U.S. have hit risk assets, but a generally weaker U.S. dollar on the back of lower yields has offered some breathing room for the Antipodeans.

"Our sense is overall the global developments are negative for the Aussie dollar," Ong said.

"Lower global trade and global growth don't bode particularly well, we would say, for the Aussie dollar. So our preference is if it moved much higher, we would probably be better sellers up towards the top of that range."

One area where there has been a breakout move are euro crosses, as Europe's common currency has shot to post-pandemic highs on the Australian and New Zealand dollars on the prospect of Ukraine and Russia agreeing to a month-long ceasefire.

Aussie 10-year bond futures (YTCc1) were steady at 95.55.

data.title: British Business - March 13
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- Britain's payment regulator, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), has dropped plans to name firms subject to an investigation following pressure from industry and government.

- Investors in global infrastructure firm Balfour Beatty are set to benefit from a share buyback and higher dividends after the group announced the departure of its chief executive.

The Guardian

- Apple's appeal against the UK government's demand to access its customers' highly encrypted data will be the subject of a secret high court hearing.

- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer could face the biggest rebellion of his premiership with dozens of Labour Party MPs angry at his plans to cut billions from the rising welfare bill and threatening to vote against freezing disability benefits.

The Telegraph

- Writing in The Telegraph, Keir Starmer admits that record tax and spending in recent years has not led to improvement in front-line services.

- Britain's biggest supermarket group Tesco is to give away food for free to shoppers as it races to slash waste and hit net zero targets, with the company to introduce "yellow sticker" prices of 0 pounds for food that is about to go out of date.

Sky News

- Keir Starmer has said he is "disappointed" to see U.S. President Donald Trump impose global tariffs on steel and aluminium, saying that the UK will take a "pragmatic approach" as it seeks an economic trade deal with the US and "all options are on the table".

- The first daily tablet for long-term treatment of endometriosis has been approved for use on Britain's National Health Service (NHS).

The Independent

- The captain of the cargo ship which collided with a U.S. oil tanker in the North Sea is a Russian national, the vessel's owner has said.

Aeris Resources Acting CFO Moves to Permanent Role; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Aeris Resources appointed Dane Van Heerden as chief financial officer in a permanent capacity, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

This follows the appointment of Van Heerden as acting CFO in August 2024.

Former CFO Rob Brainsbury remains on extended personal leave and will return to a new role, the filing stated.

Aeris Resources' shares were up 3% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Parallel Architectural Products Introduces Aluminum Self-Mating Battens
data.description:
Denver, Colorado, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Parallel Architectural Products today announced the launch of its innovative self-mating aluminum battens. This new product line builds upon the company's existing two-part battens, offering enhanced design possibilities and superior structural integrity for demanding environments.

The self-mating battens, available in multiple sizes, allow architects, designers, and contractors to use Parallel aluminum in versatile new ways — including edge installation and flat-lay configuration. These battens use identical pieces for installation, unlike traditional two-part systems, which simplifies inventory management through a single SKU.

Whether used for outdoor privacy screens, eye-catching facades, or unique interior installations, the battens create a polished, contemporary look with no visible seams or fasteners once installed. This seamless appearance eliminates costly and time-consuming installation steps and reduces maintenance needs.

The self-mating battens also feature enhanced durability through Parallel's premium aluminum construction, ensuring long-term performance in both interior and exterior applications. The system's adaptability allows for multiple attachment methods and flexible orientations, giving architects and designers greater creative freedom while maintaining structural integrity.

The addition of this product line strengthens Parallel's portfolio of contemporary, sustainable wood-look aluminum architectural solutions for commercial and residential applications. For more information about Parallel and this product launch, including technical information and product renderings, visit parallelap.com or contact epatrick@parallelap.com.

ABOUT PARALLEL ARCHITECTURAL PRODUCTS

Parallel Architectural Products has established itself as a leader in the aluminum building products industry. Our mission is to provide exceptional product quality and longevity, coupled with outstanding customer service. We offer a unique combination of timeless wood aesthetics and the durability and sustainability of aluminum, giving architects, designers, and builders the perfect blend of form and function. With our full-service team and complete control over production and finishing, we support our clients throughout the entire process—from initial concept to final completion. Our experts provide invaluable guidance on design, estimation, and technical aspects, ensuring your vision becomes a reality.

Erin Patrick

+14043681813

epatrick@parallelap.com

data.title: Singapore Dollar Strengthens Slightly Amid Lower Treasury Yields — Market Talk
data.description:
0328 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens slightly against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session amid lower Treasury yields that reduce the appeal of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. Appetite for risky assets may also have improved after U.S. inflation data released overnight were cooler than expected, analysts say. The figures have "helped appetite for risk lean forward and detach itself from the ropes it had pinned to," says Matt Simpson, market analyst at Forex.com and City Index, in a commentary. USD/SGD edges 0.1% lower to 1.3320. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield is down 2 bps at 4.2994%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0320 GMT - The latest U.S. inflation numbers look better, but the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to restart interest-rate cuts hasn't been sounded, unless there is a shock to U.S. growth, says Cedric Chehab, chief economist at BMI. A big part of the disinflation in the U.S. has been driven by declines in goods prices over recent months, he notes. Large Trump administration tariffs on goods could engineer a jump in prices very soon, which means goods will again add to inflation, he says. This makes it difficult to forecast inflation over the near term and for the Fed to provide proper guidance, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0301 GMT - Australia should leave tariff retaliation to those countries whose market size and integration can impose real costs on U.S. firms, says Lowy Institute, an independent think tank on international affairs. The cost of the tariffs imposed by the U.S. on steel and aluminum exports this week will come in at well under A$1 billion, and it is U.S. consumers that will pay much of the tariff, it says. Retaliatory action only makes sense if it hurts U.S. firms that can put pressure on the Trump administration to change its trade policies. On this front, Australia is too small a market to matter much to U.S. firms, it says. It is another reason to work constructively in the current environment with other trading partners, no matter how good it might feel to make a show of force, the institute says. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0221 GMT - When Donald Trump became president for a second time the prospects of a U.S. recession in 2025 were small, but not non-trivial. Less than two months into his term that risk seems to me better than even-money, says Stephen Miller, investment strategy advisor at GSFM. And were it to come to pass, the U.S. authorities are less equipped to deal with a recession than they have been for some time, he adds. The current Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate for current-quarter GDP is -2.4%, he notes. And even if the Fed may not be quite as constrained as feared, "sticky" inflation has robbed it of the flexibility to quickly cut rates, Miller says. The Trump agenda when it comes to things like tariffs and immigration risk a negative supply shock that makes inflation even stickier and growth more challenged. he says. And with a budget deficit approaching 7% of GDP, fiscal policy is close to maxed out, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0203 GMT - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in the morning session amid rising global trade tensions. "Elevated trade uncertainty has now become a negative for business confidence, for investment, and for growth," DBS Group Research's Chang Wei Liang says in a commentary. The outlook for U.S. economic exceptionalism could be fading, the FX & credit strategist says. The Dollar Index is consolidating around the mid-103s, getting little boost from escalating trade concerns, the strategist adds. USD/KRW edges 0.1% lower to 1,450.07; USD/SGD is little changed at 1.3326; USD/CNH is steady at 7.2396. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0136 GMT - U.S. tariffs directed at China are significant but surmountable impediments, says Elliot Clarke, head of international economics at Westpac. For the past 5 years, China has been broadening its export markets across Asia and Latin America, he says. For longer still, Chinese firms have also been expanding their production and logistic networks across Asia, particularly through southeast Asia, he adds. Not only is China able to redirect exports to non-U.S. markets, but can also produce for the U.S. outside of China to avoid tariffs, Clarke says. Even where Chinese firms cannot avoid U.S. tariffs, they are likely able to partly offset the cost of the tariffs through improved efficiency, he adds. Chinese exporters are therefore extremely well-placed to weather the economic uncertainty the U.S. is creating, Clarke says. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0035 GMT - JGBs edge higher in the morning Tokyo session, but market may be weighed by overnight price declines in U.S. Treasurys. JGBs and Treasurys tend to move in tandem. Meanwhile, some market participants could be wary of next week's BOJ meeting, which could signal an early rate increase, SMBC Nikko Securities' rates strategists say in a research report. However, even if the first round of this year's Spring wage negotiations are strong, risks of quickening inflation may not be substantially increased, so there seems to be a low probability that such as message would be sent, the strategists add. The JGB 10-year yield is down 0.5 bps at 1.515%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0014 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher as concerns about U.S. inflation ebb for now. Electronics and financial stocks are leading the gains. Advantest is up 5.3% and Rakuten Bank is 4.5% higher. USD/JPY is at 148.35, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 1.1% at 37232.93. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2351 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about U.S. inflation recede for now. Nikkei futures are up 0.9% at 37135 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 148.25, compared with 148.07 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Besides U.S. trade policy, investors are focusing on any developments in Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 36819.09 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

2157 GMT - Bank of Canada senior officials did discuss leaving its policy rate unchanged but opted for a quarter-point cut because of polling it conducted suggesting business and consumer confidence had turned sour due to tariff threats, Gov. Tiff Macklem tells Reuters. He says one scenario contemplated was keeping the policy rate at 3%. "The argument for staying at 3% was growth has been stronger than we expected," Macklem tells Reuters. During his press conference, Macklem said there was no serious thought given to a half-point cut. The fact some senior policymakers talked about leaving rates unchanged might help explain BOC's communications — which was relatively strident, or hawkish, about tariff-fueled inflation risks. The decision has traders and economists uncertain about an another cut in April.(paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

2009 GMT - Mexico's industrial production likely picked up in January after a decline in December, while remaining below year-earlier levels for a sixth consecutive month amid weakness in mining and construction. Industrial production is expected to have risen 0.4% seasonally adjusted from December, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. Production is expected to be down 1.7% unadjusted from January of 2024. Statistics institute Inegi is scheduled to report industrial production on Thursday. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1941 GMT - Treasury yields rise as inflation cools but remain above the Fed's target, amid concerns that tariffs could keep prices hot in coming months. February PPI wholesale price index is due tomorrow. The EPA announces measures to reduce red tape, promising they will unleash investment and reduce energy and other costs. A 10-year Treasury auction is met with solid demand, with a bid-to-cover ratio of 2.59, a little above the 12-month average. Jobless claims tomorrow are expected to rise slightly, in a WSJ survey. The 10-year gains 0.029 percentage point to 4.315% and the two-year rises 0.055 p.p. to 3.994%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: GCL Energy Technology to Invest 30 Million Yuan Into New Energy Fund
data.description:
GCL Energy Technology , through unit GCL Smart Energy (Suzhou), plans to invest 30.1 million yuan to establish a partnership fund, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The 10-year fund, Caithon Green New (Jiaxing) Equity Investment Partnership (Limited Partnership), will be established in partnership with Zhejiang Caithon Capital Investment, a unit of Caithon Securities.

Caithon Green will have a 50 million yuan capital and will invest in renewable energy projects, particularly green electricity and energy storage.

The Chinese polysilicon products manufacturer will own 60.1% of the fund, while Zhejiang Caithon will hold 39.9% of the fund after injecting nearly 20% into its capital.

data.title: Buchang Pharmaceuticals Gets Approval for Rabies Vaccine Clinical Trial
data.description: Shandong Buchang Pharmaceuticals subsidiary Zhejiang Tianyuan Biopharmaceutical received regulatory approval from China's drug administrator to conduct clinical trials for its freeze-dried human rabies vaccine, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Australian Foundation Investment Buys Back 310,000 Shares
data.description: Australian Foundation Investment bought back 310,000 shares for AU$2.2 million, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian and New Zealand bourses.

data.title: Ethereum Price Hits Resistance—Will The Recovery Stall Here?
data.description:
Ethereum price started a recovery wave from the $1,750 zone. ETH is now consolidating and facing hurdles near the $1,920 resistance.

  • Ethereum started a recovery wave above the $1,850 level.
  • The price is trading below $1,950 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.
  • There is a short-term bearish trend line forming with resistance at $1,920 on the hourly chart of ETH/USD (data feed via Kraken).
  • The pair must clear the $1,920 and $1,950 resistance levels to start a decent increase.

Ethereum Price Faces Hurdles

Ethereum price formed a base above the $1,750 level and started a recovery wave, like Bitcoin. ETH was able to clear the $1,800 and $1,820 resistance levels.

The bulls pushed the price above the $1,880 level. There was a move above the 23.6% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $2,150 swing high to the $1,752 low. However, the bears seem to be active near the $1,920 resistance zone.

Ethereum price is now trading below $1,950 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average. On the upside, the price seems to be facing hurdles near the $1,920 level.

There is also a short-term bearish trend line forming with resistance at $1,920 on the hourly chart of ETH/USD. The next key resistance is near the $1,950 level or the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $2,150 swing high to the $1,752 low.

The first major resistance is near the $2,000 level. A clear move above the $2,000 resistance might send the price toward the $2,060 resistance. An upside break above the $2,060 resistance might call for more gains in the coming sessions. In the stated case, Ether could rise toward the $2,120 resistance zone or even $2,250 in the near term.

Another Decline In ETH?

If Ethereum fails to clear the $1,920 resistance, it could start another decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $1,850 level. The first major support sits near the $1,800 zone.

A clear move below the $1,800 support might push the price toward the $1,750 support. Any more losses might send the price toward the $1,720 support level in the near term. The next key support sits at $1,650.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for ETH/USD is losing momentum in the bearish zone.

Hourly RSI – The RSI for ETH/USD is now above the 50 zone.

Major Support Level – $1,800

Major Resistance Level – $1,920
